name: pynitrokey

metadata.name: pynitrokey
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# pynitrokey

A command line interface for the Nitrokey FIDO2, Nitrokey Start, Nitrokey 3 and NetHSM.

## Quickstart

```
$ pipx install pynitrokey
$ nitropy --help
```

## Documentation

The user documentation for the `nitropy` CLI is available on [docs.nitrokey.com](https://docs.nitrokey.com/software/nitropy/index.html). See also the product documentation for more information on the available commands:
- [Nitrokey 3](https://docs.nitrokey.com/nitrokey3/index.html)
- [Nitrokey FIDO2](https://docs.nitrokey.com/fido2/index.html)
- [Nitrokey Start](https://docs.nitrokey.com/start/index.html)
- [NetHSM](https://docs.nitrokey.com/nethsm/index.html)

### Switching Nitrokey Start identities

#### Alternative MI switching method

<details>

`pynitrokey` installation is not always possible, hence describing below alternative method to change the Identity on the Nitrokey Start. It suffices to have any CCID application installed, and send the following APDU `00 85 00 {ID}` (hex), where `ID` is in range `[0;2]`. After receiving this command Nitrokey Start will reboot with the selected identity.

Here is how to do it using GnuPG:
```text
# Setting ID to 2
$ gpg-connect-agent --hex "scd apdu  00 85 00 02" /bye
ERR 65539 Unknown version in packet <Unspecified source>

# Alternative error messsage
ERR 65572 Bad certificate <Unspecified source>
```

The error message here is expected due to immediate reboot of the device, and with losing the connection.

When the ID change is attempted to be done immediately, the following response could be received:
```
ERR 100663406 Card removed <SCD>
```
To restore the communication, either kill the `gpg-agent` or run `gpg --card-status` again.

Tip: alternative `gpg-connect-agent reloadagent /bye` is not sufficient.
</details>

## Compatibility

`nitropy` requires Python 3.9 or later.

## Development

Information for developers and contributors can be found in the [Developer Guide](./docs/developer-guide.rst).

## Contributors

`pynitrokey` development has been supported by these contributors:
- [@Laborratte5](https://github.com/Laborratte5)
- [@runcom](https://github.com/runcom)

## Maintainers

Current maintainers can be found in [MAINTAINERS.md](MAINTAINERS.md) file.

functions.name: pynitrokey.fido2.device_path_to_str
documentation:
Converts a device path as returned by the fido2 library to a string.

Typically, the path already is a string.  Only on Windows, a bytes object
using an ANSI encoding is used instead.  We use the ISO 8859-1 encoding to
decode the string which should work for all systems.

classes.name: pynitrokey.helpers.AskUser
documentation:
Asking user for input:
    `question`:       printed user question
    `options`:        `[]`        -> we want some data input
                      `List[str]` -> only allow items inside iterable
    `strict`:         if `options` are used, force full match
    `repeat`:         ask `question` up to `repeat` times, if `options` are provided
    `adapt_question`: adapt user-provided `question` (add options, whitespace...),
                      set to `False`, if strictly `question` shall be used
    `hide_input`:     use 'getpass' instead of regular `input`
@fixme: consider using/wrapping click.confirm() instead of this...

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.program.bootloader
documentation:
Program via Nitrokey FIDO2 bootloader interface.

 
FIRMWARE argument should be either a .hex or .json file.

If the bootloader is verifying, the .json is needed containing
a signature for the verifying key in the bootloader.

If the bootloader is nonverifying, either .hex or .json can be used.

DANGER: if you try to flash a firmware with signature that doesn't
match the bootloader's verifying key, you will be stuck in bootloader
mode until you find a signed firmware that does match.

Enter bootloader mode using `nitropy fido2 util program aux enter-bootloader` first.
@click.command()
@click.option("-s", "--serial", help="Serial number of Nitrokey to use")
@click.argument("firmware")  # , help="firmware (bundle) to program")
def check_only(serial, firmware):
"""Validate currently flashed firmware, and run on success. Bootloader only."""
from pynitrokey.fido2 import find
p = find(serial)
try:
p.use_hid()
p.program_file(firmware)
except CtapError as e:
if e.code == CtapError.ERR.INVALID_COMMAND:
local_critical("Not in bootloader mode.", e)

classes.name: pynitrokey.libnk.RetCode
documentation: def lazyio(f):
@wraps
def wrapper(*v, **kw):
ret_val = f(*v, **kw)
return py_enc(ret_val) \
if isinstance(ret_val, ffi.CData) and \
"char" in ffi.typeof(ret_val).cname else ret_val
return wrapper

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nk3.nk3
documentation: Interact with Nitrokey 3 devices, see subcommands.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nk3.update
documentation:
Update the firmware of the device using the given image.

This command requires that exactly one Nitrokey 3 in bootloader or firmware mode is connected.
The user is asked to confirm the operation before the update is started.  If the --confirm
option is provided, this is the confirmation.  This option may be used to automate an update.
The Nitrokey 3 may not be removed during the update.  Also, additional Nitrokey 3 devices may
not be connected during the update.

If no firmware image is given, the latest firmware release is downloaded automatically.  If
the --version option is set, the given version is downloaded instead.

If the connected Nitrokey 3 device is in firmware mode, the user is prompted to touch the
device’s button to confirm rebooting to bootloader mode.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nk3.get_config
documentation: Query a config value.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nk3.set_config
documentation:
Set a config value.

Per default, this command can only be used with configuration values that
are known to pynitrokey.  Changing some configuration values can have side
effects.  For these values, a summary of the effects of the change and a
confirmation prompt will be printed.

If you use the --force/-f flag, you can also set configuration values that
are not known to pynitrokey.  This may have unexpected side effects, for
example resetting an application.  It is only intended for development and
testing.

To see the information about a config value without actually performing the
change, use the --dry-run flag.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.fido2.genkey
documentation:
Generates key pair that can be used for Solo signed firmware updates.

 
* Generates NIST P256 keypair.
* Public key must be copied into correct source location in solo bootloader
* The private key can be used for signing updates.
* You may optionally supply a file to seed the RNG for key generating.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.fido2.make_credential
documentation:
Generate a credential.

Pass `--prompt ""` to output only the `credential_id` as hex.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.fido2.challenge_response
documentation:
Uses `hmac-secret` to implement a challenge-response mechanism.

We abuse hmac-secret, which gives us `HMAC(K, hash(challenge))`, where `K`
is a secret tied to the `credential_id`. We hash the challenge first, since
a 32 byte value is expected (in original usage, it's a salt).

This means that we first need to setup a credential_id; this depends on the
specific authenticator used. To do this, use `nitropy fido2 make-credential`.

If so desired, user and relying party can be changed from the defaults.

The prompt can be suppressed using `--prompt ""`.

documentation: Version of firmware on device.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.fido2.wink
documentation: Send wink command to device (blinks LED a few times).

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.fido2.reboot
documentation: Send reboot command to device (development command)

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.version
documentation: Version of pynitrokey library and tool.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.list
documentation: List Nitrokey devices (in firmware or bootloader mode)

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nk3.secrets.secrets
documentation:
Nitrokey Secrets App. Manage OTP and Password Safe secrets on the device.
Use NITROPY_SECRETS_PASSWORD to pass password for the scripted execution.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nk3.secrets.repeat_if_pin_needed
documentation:
Repeat the call of the decorated function, if PIN is required.
Decorated function should have at least one argument,
of which the first one should be an instance of the SecretsApp. Otherwise, a RuntimeError is raised.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nk3.secrets.rename
documentation: Rename credential.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nk3.secrets.update
documentation: Update credential. Change Static Password fields, or touch button requirement attribute.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nk3.secrets.add_otp
documentation:
Register OTP credential.

Write credential under the NAME.
Secret should be base32 encoded.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nk3.secrets.add_password
documentation:
Register Password Safe credential.

Write credential under the NAME.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nk3.secrets.add_challenge_response
documentation: Register Challenge-Response credential.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nk3.secrets.ask_to_touch_if_needed
documentation: Helper function to show common request for the touch if device signalizes it

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nk3.secrets.list
documentation: List registered OTP credentials.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nk3.secrets.remove
documentation: Remove OTP credential.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nk3.secrets.reset
documentation: Remove all OTP credentials from the device.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nk3.secrets.get_otp
documentation: Generate OTP code from registered credential.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nk3.secrets.get_password
documentation: Get Password Safe Entry

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nk3.secrets.verify
documentation:
Proceed with the incoming OTP code verification (aka reverse HOTP).
Use the "register" command to create the credential for this action.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nk3.secrets.set_pin
documentation: Set or change the PIN used to authenticate to other commands.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nk3.secrets.status
documentation: Show application status

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.storage.storage
documentation: Interact with Nitrokey Storage devices, see subcommands.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.storage.process_runner
documentation: Wrapper for running command and returning output, both logged

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.storage.update
documentation: experimental: run assisted update through dfu-programmer tool

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.storage.list
documentation: List connected devices

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.storage.enable_update
documentation:
Enable firmware update for NK Storage device

If the Firmware Password is not in the environment variable NITROPY_FIRMWARE_PASSWORD, it will be prompted from stdin

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.storage.change_firmware_password
documentation:
Change the firmware update password.

The user is prompted for the old and the new firmware update password.  Per
default, the firmware update password is 12345678.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.storage.open_encrypted
documentation:
Unlock the encrypted volume

If the User PIN is not in the environment variable NITROPY_USER_PIN, it will be prompted from stdin

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.storage.close_encrypted
documentation: Lock the encrypted volume

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.storage.open_hidden
documentation:
Unlock a hidden volume

If the hidden volume passphrase is not in the environment variable NITROPY_HIDDEN_PASSPHRASE, it will be prompted from stdin

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.storage.close_hidden
documentation: Lock the hidden volumes

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.storage.create_hidden
documentation:
Create a hidden volume

SLOT is the slot used for the hidden volume (1-4)

START is where the volume begins expressed in percent of total available storage (0-99)

END is where the volume ends expressed in percent of total available storage (1-100)

If the hidden volume passphrase is not in the environment variable NITROPY_HIDDEN_PASSPHRASE, it will be prompted from stdin

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.storage.check
documentation:
Check if provided binary image contains user data in the proper region

Use it on downloaded full image with `--force` flag, as in: 

$ dfu-programmer at32uc3a3256s read --bin --force > dump.bin

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.storage.compare
documentation: Compare two binary images

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.storage.merge
documentation: Simple tool to merge user data into the new firmware binary

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.storage.user_data
documentation: experimental: commands to check and manipulate user data in the downloaded binary images

methods.name: pynitrokey.cli.storage.DfuTool.is_available
documentation: Check whether `name` is on PATH and marked as executable.

class_variables.name: pynitrokey.cli.storage.MemoryConstants.USER_DATA_START
documentation: 0x3DE00

class_variables.name: pynitrokey.cli.storage.MemoryConstants.USER_DATA_END
documentation: 0x3FE00

modules.name: pynitrokey.cli.trussed
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
Copyright 2021-2024 Nitrokey Developers
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0, <LICENSE-APACHE or
http://apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0> or the MIT license <LICENSE-MIT or
http://opensource.org/licenses/MIT>, at your option. This file may not be
copied, modified, or distributed except according to those terms.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.trussed.fetch_update
documentation:
Fetches a firmware update and stores it at the given path.

If no path is given, the firmware image stored in the current working
directory.  If the given path is a directory, the image is stored under
that directory.  Otherwise it is written to the path.  Existing files are
only overwritten if --force is set.

Per default, the latest firmware release is fetched.  If you want to
download a specific version, use the --version option.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.trussed.list
documentation: List all devices.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.trussed.provision
documentation:
Provision the device.  This command is only used during the production
process and not available for regular devices.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.trussed.provision_fido2
documentation: Provision the FIDO2 attestation key and certificate.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.trussed.reboot
documentation:
Reboot the key.

Per default, the key will reboot into regular firmware mode.  If the --bootloader option
is set, a key can boot from firmware mode to bootloader mode.  Booting into
bootloader mode has to be confirmed by pressing the touch button.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.trussed.rng
documentation: Generate random data on the device.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.trussed.status
documentation: Query the device status.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.trussed.test
documentation: Run some tests on all connected devices.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.trussed.validate_update
documentation:
Validates the given firmware image and prints the firmware version and the signer for all
available variants.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.trussed.version
documentation: Query the firmware version of the device.

classes.name: pynitrokey.start.gnuk_token.gnuk_token

methods.name: pynitrokey.start.gnuk_token.gnuk_token.__init__
documentation:
__init__(device, configuration, interface) -> None
Initialize the device.
device: usb.Device object.
configuration: configuration number.
interface: usb.Interface object representing the interface and altenate setting.

methods.name: pynitrokey.start.gnuk_token.gnuk_token.release_on_exit
documentation:
Release GNUK device once finished, to not block other smart card services
trying to claim it
This class only supports Gnuk (for now)

modules.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
Copyright 2021 Nitrokey Developers
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0, <LICENSE-APACHE or
http://apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0> or the MIT license <LICENSE-MIT or
http://opensource.org/licenses/MIT>, at your option. This file may not be
copied, modified, or distributed except according to those terms.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.nethsm
documentation: Interact with NetHSM devices, see subcommands.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.unlock
documentation: Bring a locked NetHSM into operational state.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.lock
documentation:
Bring an operational NetHSM into locked state.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.provision
documentation:
Initial provisioning of a NetHSM.

If the unlock or admin passphrases are not set, they have to be entered
interactively.  If the system time is not set, the current system time is
used.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.list_users
documentation:
List all users on the NetHSM.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.get_user
documentation:
Query the real name and role for a user ID on the NetHSM.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator or
Operator role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.add_user
documentation:
Create a new user on the NetHSM.

If the real name, role or passphrase are not specified, they have to be
specified interactively.  If the user ID is not set, it is generated by the
NetHSM.

If a namespace is specified, the user will be created within the namespace.
This means that the resulting user name will follow the pattern
namespace~userid, i. e. the same user ID can be used in different
namespaces.

If the --create-namespace option is set and a namespace is specified, the
namespace will be created after the user has been added.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.delete_user
documentation:
Delete the user with the given user ID on the NetHSM.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.list_namespaces
documentation:
List all namespaces on the NetHSM.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.add_namespace
documentation:
Add a new namespace on the NetHSM.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.delete_namespace
documentation:
Delete a namespace on the NetHSM.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.set_passphrase
documentation:
Set the passphrase for the user with the given ID (or the current user).

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator or
Operator role.  Users with the Operator role can only change their own
passphrase.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.list_operator_tags
documentation:
List the tags for an operator user ID on the NetHSM.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.add_operator_tag
documentation:
Add a tag for an operator user on the NetHSM.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.delete_operator_tag
documentation:
Delete a tag for an operator user on the NetHSM.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.add_key_tag
documentation:
Add a tag for a key on the NetHSM.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.delete_key_tag
documentation:
Delete a tag for a key on the NetHSM.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.info
documentation: Query the vendor and product information for a NetHSM.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.state
documentation: Query the state of a NetHSM.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.random
documentation:
Retrieve random bytes from the NetHSM as a Base64 string.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Operator role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.metrics
documentation:
Query the metrics of a NetHSM.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Metrics role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.list_keys
documentation:
List all keys on the NetHSM.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator or
Operator role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.get_key
documentation:
Get information about a key on the NetHSM.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator or
Operator role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.delete_key
documentation:
Delete the key pair with the given key ID on the NetHSM.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.add_key
documentation:
Add a key pair on the NetHSM.

If the key ID is not set, it is generated by the NetHSM.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.import_key
documentation:
Import a key pair from a PEM file into the NetHSM.

If the key ID is not set, it is generated by the NetHSM.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.generate_key
documentation:
Generate a key pair on the NetHSM.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.get_config
documentation:
Query the configuration of a NetHSM.

Only the configuration items selected with the corresponding option are
queried.  If no option is set, all items are queried.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.set_backup_passphrase
documentation:
Set the backup passphrase of a NetHSM.

Changing the backup passphrase requires the current passphrase (if set,
empty string otherwise).

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.set_unlock_passphrase
documentation:
Set the unlock passphrase of a NetHSM.

Changing the unlock passphrase requires the current passphrase.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.set_logging_config
documentation:
Set the logging configuration of a NetHSM.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.set_network_config
documentation:
Set the network configuration of a NetHSM.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.set_time
documentation:
Set the system time of a NetHSM.

If the time is not given as an argument, the system time of this system is used.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.set_unattended_boot
documentation:
Set the unattended boot configuration of a NetHSM.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.get_api_or_key_id
documentation:
Helper method for operations that can be executed either for the API
certificate or for the certificate stored for a key.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.set_certificate
documentation:
Set a certificate on the NetHSM.

If the --api option is set, the certificate used for the NetHSM TLS interface
is set.  If the --key-id option is set, the certificate for a key stored on
the NetHSM is set.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.get_certificate
documentation:
Get a certificate from the NetHSM.

If the --api option is set, the certificate used for the NetHSM TLS interface
is queried.  If the --key-id option is set, the certificate for a key stored on
the NetHSM is queried.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator role.
The certificate for a key can also be queried by a user with the Operator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.delete_certificate
documentation:
Delete a certificate for a stored key from the NetHSM.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.csr
documentation:
Generate a certificate signing request.

If the --api option is set, the CSR is generated for the NetHSM, for
example to replace the self-signed initial certificate.  If the --key-id
option is set, the CSR is generated for a key stored on the NetHSM.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.generate_tls_key
documentation:
Generate key pair for NetHSM TLS interface.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.system_info
documentation:
Get system information for a NetHSM instance.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.backup
documentation:
Make a backup of a NetHSM instance and write it to a file.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Backup role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.restore
documentation:
Restore a backup of a NetHSM instance from a file.

If the system time is not set, the current system time is used.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.validate_backup
documentation:
Validate a NetHSM backup file.

Per default, only the metadata of the encrypted backup is validated.  If
the backup passphrase is set, the backup is decrypted and the content is
also validated.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.export_backup
documentation:
Export the content of a NetHSM backup file.

The key-value data stored in the backup file is printed to the standard
output as a JSON object using the base64 encoding for binary data.
Additionally, the .locked-domain-key and .version keys are set with the
domain key and version info extracted from the backup file.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.update
documentation:
Load an update to a NetHSM instance.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.cancel_update
documentation:
Cancel a queued update on a NetHSM instance.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.commit_update
documentation:
Commit a queued update on a NetHSM instance.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.reboot
documentation:
Reboot a NetHSM instance.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.shutdown
documentation:
Shutdown a NetHSM instance.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.factory_reset
documentation:
Perform a factory reset for a NetHSM instance.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Administrator
role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.encrypt
documentation:
Encrypt data with an asymmetric secret key on the NetHSM and print the encrypted message.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Operator role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.decrypt
documentation:
Decrypt data with a secret key on the NetHSM and print the decrypted message.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Operator role.

functions.name: pynitrokey.cli.nethsm.sign
documentation:
Sign data with a secret key on the NetHSM and print the signature.

This command requires authentication as a user with the Operator role.

modules.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app
documentation:
Tests for the Secrets application interface placed in secrets_app.py.
Requires a live device, or an USB-IP simulation.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_reset
documentation: Clear credentials' storage. Simple test.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_list
documentation: List saved credentials. Simple test.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_register
documentation: Register credential with the given id and properties. Simple test.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_calculate
documentation: Run calculation on the default credential id. Simple test.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_delete
documentation: Remove credential with the given id. Simple test.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_delete_nonexisting
documentation: Should fail when trying to remove non-existing credential id.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_list_changes
documentation: Test how the list of credential changes, when one is added or removed, and after a reset.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_calculated_codes_hotp
documentation:
Test HOTP codes against another OTP library.
Use different secret and start counter values.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_calculated_codes_totp
documentation: Test TOTP codes against another OTP library.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_calculated_codes_test_vector
documentation:
Check output against RFC4226 test vectors, as provided in
https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc4226#page-32

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_reverse_hotp_vectors
documentation:
Test passing conditions for the HOTP reverse check
Check against RFC4226 test vectors, as provided in
https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc4226#page-32

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_reverse_hotp_failure
documentation: Test failing conditions for the HOTP reverse check

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_reverse_hotp_window
documentation:
Test reverse HOTP code calculation synchronization.
Solution contains a means to avoid desynchronization between the host's and device's counters. Device calculates
up to 9 values ahead of its current counter to find the matching code (in total it calculates HOTP code for 10
subsequent counter positions). In case:

 - no code would match - the on-device counter will not be changed;
 - code would match, but with some counter's offset (up to 9) - the on-device counter will be set to matched code-generated HOTP counter and incremented by 1;
 - code would match, and the code matches counter without offset - the counter will be incremented by 1;
 - counter overflows while calculating the code within the specified window - error is returned, and in that case a new credential with reset counter should be registered

Device will stop verifying the HOTP codes in case, when the difference between the host and on-device counters
will be greater or equal to 10.
See https://github.com/Nitrokey/nitrokey-hotp-verification#verifying-hotp-code for more information.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_calculated_codes_totp_hash_digits
documentation:
Test TOTP codes against another OTP library, with different hash algorithms and digits count.
Test vector secret, and a random 40 bytes value.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_load
documentation:
Load tests to see how much OTP credentials we can store,
and if using of them is not broken with the full FS.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_remove_all_credentials_by_hand
documentation: Remove all hold credentials by hand and test for being empty.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_send_rubbish
documentation: Check if the application crashes, when sending unexpected data for the given command

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_too_long_message
documentation: Check device's response for the too long message

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_too_long_message2
documentation: Test how long the secret could be

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_status
documentation: Simple test for getting device's status

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_set_code
documentation: Simple test for setting the proper code on the device.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_set_code_and_validate
documentation:
Test device's behavior when the validation code is set.
Non-authorized calls should be rejected, except for the selected.
Authorization should be valid only until the next call.

Authorization is needed for all the listed commands, except for RESET and VALIDATE:
     Required               Not required
     PUT 0x01               RESET 0x04 N
     DELETE 0x02            VALIDATE 0xa3 N
     SET CODE 0x03
     LIST 0xa1
     CALCULATE 0xa2
     CALCULATE ALL 0xa4
     SEND REMAINING 0xa5
Details:
- https://developers.yubico.com/OATH/YKOATH_Protocol.html

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_revhotp_bruteforce
documentation:
This test implements practical brute-forcing of the codes values.
In case multiple devices use the same secret, stealing and brute-forcing answers on one
could help with the other.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_revhotp_delay_on_failure
documentation:
Check if the right delay is set, when the invalid code is given for the reverse HOTP operation.
On failure the response time should take at least 1 second to prevent easy brute force.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_set_pin
documentation: Simple test for setting the PIN on the device.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_change_pin
documentation: Simple test for setting the proper code on the device.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_change_pin_data_dont_change
documentation:
Test for changing the proper PIN on the device.
Check if data remain the same, both PIN and Hardware key encrypted

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_verify_pin
documentation: Simple test for PIN verification

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_list_pin_no_pin
documentation:
Check list command behavior in the presence of PIN and without it
1. Credential list should be empty after reset
2. Credential list should show only non-PIN-encrypted credentials, when unauthorized
3. Credential list should show both non- and PIN-encrypted credentials, when authorized
@param secretsAppRaw: Direct access to the Secret App API, without any additional setup

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_check_commands_access
documentation:
Access to all commands should be always available. Sending garbage, and expecting parsing error.
If this was access guarded, the parsing would not happen.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_register_pin_encrypted_without_auth
documentation: PIN-encrypted Credential registration should not be possible without previous verification

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_pin_operations_do_not_authenticate
documentation: Make sure that PIN operations other than Verify do not authenticate

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_credential_encryption_does_not_change
documentation: Check if counter-updated HOTP Credential does not change its encryption type after use.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.helper_send_receive_ins
pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_password_safe
documentation: Create a full credential, with both OTP and PWS fields populated. Test working both, with and without PIN-based encryption.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_password_safe_empty_credential
documentation: It should be possible to create an empty credential, with just the name presented

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_password_safe_just_pws_entry
documentation: It should be possible to create a PWS-only credential

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_select_applet
documentation: Low-level test for testing the response of the select command

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.helper_get_padded
documentation: Get PKCS#7 padded buffer

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_hmac_low_level
documentation:
Test HMAC Challenge setup and use, for KeepassXC support.
Low-level test.
Support for this feature is not planned to be added in the SecretsApp API.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_list_with_properties
pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_light_load
documentation:
Add a couple of different Credentials' types for the manual CLI listing tests.
When `NKPRE` env variable is set, the factory reset is not called, and Credentials
are prefixed with this string.
Example call:
```
env NKPRE=0.12-2- make secrets-test LOG=debug TESTADD="-k light"
```

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_register_overwrite_attempt
documentation: Credential should not be overwritten with another one, regardless whether it is PIN-encrypted or not

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_rename_credential
documentation: Credential should change its name. Test both PIN- and HW-encrypted credentials.

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_rename_credential_to_existing
documentation: Credential should not change its name to an existing one, regardless if the other is PIN-encrypted or not

functions.name: pynitrokey.test_secrets_app.test_update_credential
documentation: Credential should change its properties. Test both PIN- and HW-encrypted credentials.
